user_query:
I'm a Coptic historian preparing a bilingual glossary of core Coptic hagiographic titles for an article. Using the Arabic titles in input/titles_arabic.csv, please do the following:

1) Inspect the configuration files at config/schema.yaml and config/allowed_sources.yaml before you start. The schema file defines the exact fields and constraints for the outputs, and the allowed-sources file restricts which sites can be used as authoritative references.

2) For each Arabic title, perform search engine queries on the Internet (at least two distinct queries per title, in Arabic and/or English) to locate an authoritative English title on a source listed under allowed in config/allowed_sources.yaml. If you find a suitable authoritative source, use that English title and record the citation details. If no suitable authoritative source is found, produce your own literal English translation and mark it as an agent translation as defined in the schema.

3) Write two outputs using the field names and formats exactly as defined in config/schema.yaml:
   - out/glossary.csv: one row per input title, preserving the input order. Include english_title, translation_type (authoritative or agent_translation), and for authoritative cases include source_name and source_url. For agent translations, leave those source fields empty and add a short note indicating that no allowed source was found.
   - out/search_log.jsonl: one JSON object per input title capturing the queries you used, the chosen source (if any), and at least two alternative candidates considered with their titles and URLs.

Additional requirements:
- Every input title must appear exactly once in out/glossary.csv.
- If translation_type is authoritative, the domain of source_url must match one of the domain_patterns specified for an allowed source in config/allowed_sources.yaml.
- Do not use banned sources listed in config/allowed_sources.yaml.

Please rely on the configuration files for field names, required fields, and validation rules, and keep both outputs consistent with those definitions. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
